seo.title: DSPy Guardrails for E-Commerce
seo.description: Compliance guide for DSPy agents in e-commerce. Meet FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: E-Commerce teams using DSPy to optimize LLM-powered product recommenders, pricing engines, and customer service bots face unique compliance risks due to the framework's optimization tendencies. DSPy's signature-based optimization can lead to overfitting prompts that generate unsubstantiated product claims or inaccurate pricing, violating FTC guidelines and PCI DSS. Vex provides runtime verification to catch these failures before they reach customers, ensuring regulatory compliance and protecting revenue.

complianceRequirements.description: Product claims must be substantiated with evidence; optimized DSPy chains can hallucinate benefits that violate truth-in-advertising laws.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies product claims against a knowledge base of verified specs during inference, blocking unsubstantiated outputs.

complianceRequirements.description: Customer data handling in personalization modules must respect consent and deletion requests; DSPy optimizers may retain sensitive patterns.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces data minimization assertions on recommendation modules, redacting PII and validating consent flows.

complianceRequirements.description: Pricing and payment flows must display accurate totals; DSPy chain-of-thought can miscalculate discounts or taxes.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex wraps pricing modules with arithmetic assertions, catching optimization-induced calculation errors before checkout.

complianceRequirements.description: Product descriptions and chat responses must be accessible; DSPy modules may generate complex language or alt-text failures.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates outputs against WCAG readability scores and semantic accessibility checks at runtime.

industryRisks.name: Overstated Product Claims
industryRisks.description: DSPy optimization overfits to training examples, generating claims like 'best-rated blender' without evidence.
industryRisks.example: Customer sues for false advertising after blender underperforms, triggering FTC investigation.

industryRisks.name: Pricing Miscalculations
industryRisks.description: Chain-of-thought reasoning in discount modules produces plausible but incorrect final prices.
industryRisks.example: Checkout shows $50 instead of $500 item, leading to $1M+ refund disputes.

industryRisks.name: Inaccurate Refund Policy
industryRisks.description: Module composition alters return policy representations across customer service chains.
industryRisks.example: Chatbot promises 90-day returns (actual: 30 days), causing customer churn and chargebacks.

industryRisks.name: PII Leakage in Recommendations
industryRisks.description: Optimized personalization modules fail to redact sensitive data from prior interactions.
industryRisks.example: Recommendation email exposes 'John's recent divorce inquiry', violating CCPA with $7,500 per violation fines.

implementation.title: Verify Product Claims in DSPy Signature
implementation.description: Wrap DSPy's product description generator with Vex to ensure claims match verified spec sheets.

implementation.title: PCI-Compliant Pricing Calculator
implementation.description: Protect checkout flows by verifying DSPy pricing chain arithmetic before rendering to customer.

implementation.title: CCPA-Safe Personalization Module
implementation.description: Ensure DSPy recommenders strip PII and respect opt-out signals using Vex assertions.

caseStudyScenario.heading: DSPy in E-Commerce: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
FashionCo deployed DSPy to power their personalized styling assistant, optimizing a ChainOfThought module that generates outfit recommendations with product descriptions, pricing, and return policy summaries. During the two-week optimization phase using their historical chat data, the module achieved 92% signature match rate on validation. However, when deployed to production, customers began complaining about 'guaranteed perfect fit' claims on clothing items and checkout pages showing inconsistent pricing after bundle discounts. The return policy summaries also started mentioning 'lifetime returns' instead of the actual 30-day policy. Within 48 hours, negative reviews spiked 300%, triggering FTC complaint monitoring and a class-action inquiry.

The root cause was DSPy optimization overfitting to training conversations containing customer exaggerations, combined with chain composition propagating errors across modules. Without runtime checks, these failures reached 15% of checkout sessions.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: FashionCo integrated Vex verification wrapping their DSPy forward() calls within 4 hours. The product claim verifier blocked 98% of unsubstantiated descriptions, pricing assertions caught all arithmetic errors, and policy summaries were locked to verified templates. Customer complaints dropped to zero within 24 hours, FTC alerts cleared automatically, and conversion rates improved 12% due to trustworthy messaging. Vex's assertion logs provided audit trails proving compliance during regulatory review.

cta.heading: Comply with E-Commerce Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK today and wrap your DSPy modules to eliminate compliance failures before they impact revenue.
